name: Fudge-Filled Cream Wafers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Line cookie sheets with parchment paper or prepare ungreased baking sheets.
3. In a large bowl, blend the softened butter into the flour until it forms pea-size pieces.
4. Drizzle cream over the mixture; toss by hand just until mixed (mixture will look curdled).
5. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface and press together to form a ball.
6. If dough is too soft to handle, cover and refrigerate until firm.
7. Roll out dough in thirds, about 1/8 inch thick on a lightly floured surface.
8. Cut with a 1 1/4-inch round cutter. Dip the top of each dough circle in granulated sugar.
9. Place sugared sides up, about 2 inches apart, on prepared cookie sheets.
10. Pierce each cookie with a fork.
11. Bake for 9-11 minutes or until edges are very lightly browned. Remove to wire racks to cool.
FUDGE FILLING:
1. Melt the butter and chocolate in a small bowl over hot water.
2. Stir until smooth. Blend in powdered sugar and vanilla.
3. Stir in coffee until filling is spreadable, but not too soft.
